message:
feat(protocol, server): implement ToolAnnotations for MCP tool metadata

Add ToolAnnotations struct to fastmcp-protocol/types.rs:
- destructive: bool - indicates tool may cause side effects (e.g., delete files)
- idempotent: bool - safe to retry without changing outcome
- read_only: bool - tool only reads data without modifications
- open_world_hint: String - behavior hint for unknown inputs
- Builder pattern with fluent API (destructive(), idempotent(), read_only(),
  open_world_hint() methods)
- is_empty() method to check if any annotation is set
- Proper serde attributes for camelCase serialization and skip_serializing_if

Update Tool struct to include optional annotations field

Update fastmcp-server/handler.rs:
- Add annotations() method to ToolHandler trait (default returns None)
- Implement annotations() forwarding in MountedToolHandler
- Documentation explaining annotations can be set via trait or definition()

Update console display (handlers.rs, tables.rs):
- Wire through annotation display support for console output

These annotations align with MCP protocol best practices for providing
behavioral hints to clients, enabling better tool usage decisions and
safety checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
